steps.title: Break the charge into two pieces
steps.content: The first $25$ people cost $\$28$ each, a fixed amount of $25\cdot 28=\$700$. Anyone beyond that is charged $\$17$ each, and there are $n-25$ such people when $n\ge 25$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $17n+275$: gives $\$700$ at $n=25$ and adds $\$17$ per extra person. Matches.
  • B) $17n+28$: at $n=25$ this is $\$453$, far less than $\$700$. Eliminated.
  • C) $17n+700$: at $n=25$ this is $\$1{,}125$, overcharging the base group. Eliminated.
  • D) $45n-425$: at $n=25$ this is $\$700$ (coincidence), but slope is $\$45$/person, not $\$17$. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
